starlink_position: Very high — 10M+ existing subscribers, 7 years of word-of-mouth, extensive media coverage
amazon_leo_position: Low-moderate — Amazon brand is trusted broadly, but 'Leo'/'Kuiper' name recognition is near zero among consumers
weight_rationale: Familiarity reduces perceived risk in a high-cost

starlink_position: Strong — reviews, YouTube unboxings, rural community adoption stories, 7 years of public track record
amazon_leo_position: None yet — zero consumer reviews, zero installed base to reference
weight_rationale: New entrants without social proof must compete on price or exclusive access

starlink_position: High hardware sunk cost ($349) creates lock-in for current subscribers
amazon_leo_position: Must overcome existing Starlink hardware sunk cost to win switchers
weight_rationale: Amazon Leo's early addressable market is mostly new-to-category or badly-served Starlink customers

amazon_leo_position: Higher — no publicly available uptime, latency, or customer service track record
weight_rationale: Classic uncertainty-reduction problem for any second mover

starlink_position: Massive constellation enables broad simultaneous coverage claims
amazon_leo_position: Limited coverage until ~578 satellites reached; Amazon has said this is required for continuous service across initial latitudes
weight_rationale: Coverage claims are not yet verifiable for Leo in most target markets

amazon_leo_position: Potential to bundle with Amazon Prime membership (unconfirmed but widely speculated)
weight_rationale: Bundling could be Amazon's strongest lever to overcome cold-start brand problem

starlink_position: Fully published, tiered, publicly compared by review sites
amazon_leo_position: Not yet announced as of July 2026
weight_rationale: Uncertainty about price itself is a friction point until Amazon publishes rates

starlink_position: US government/DoD contracts; T-Mobile direct-to-cell partnership
amazon_leo_position: JetBlue, Delta, AT&T, Vodafone enterprise deals signal credibility before consumer launch
weight_rationale: Amazon appears to be sequencing B2B trust-building ahead of B2C launch

amazon_leo_position: Potential to leverage Amazon.com storefront and existing logistics/fulfillment network
weight_rationale: Amazon's retail distribution muscle is a structural advantage Starlink cannot easily replicate

starlink_position: Generally seen as on-track with FCC obligations
amazon_leo_position: Requested FCC deadline extension; some public narrative about falling behind schedule
weight_rationale: Public perception of program risk could suppress early sign-ups if not managed proactively

starlink_position: First mover; defined the category and consumer expectations
amazon_leo_position: Fast follower with the advantage of Starlink's proven demand and known objections to solve for
weight_rationale: Fast followers can win if they solve a specific known pain point Starlink has (e.g., congestion pricing, customer service)
